messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: Thank you for calling AT&T, my name is Sarah. How can I assist you today?

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: Hi Sarah. Yeah, I'm calling because I need to cancel my AT&T internet service. It's just gotten too expensive.

messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: I understand, I'm sorry to hear that. Before we proceed, could you tell me a little more about what's making you consider canceling? Is it just the cost, or are there other issues with your service?

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: Honestly, it's pretty much just the cost. I've been a customer for years, but my bill keeps creeping up, and frankly, I've seen much better deals from other providers in my area. I just can't justify paying this much anymore.

messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: I completely understand that, and we definitely value your loyalty. Let me see what options I have available to keep you as part of the AT&T family. Looking at your account, I can offer you a promotional discount that would bring your internet bill down to $60 a month for the next 12 months, which is a significant saving from your current rate. Would that be something you'd be interested in?

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: Hmm, $60... I appreciate that, Sarah, really. But honestly, I've already signed up for a new provider that's offering me faster speeds for $50 a month, guaranteed for two years. So, even with that discount, it's still more expensive than what I'm getting elsewhere, and for less speed. I really wish this offer had come sooner.

messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: I see. I'm truly sorry we couldn't match that offer for you. In that case, I can certainly process the cancellation of your AT&T internet service. Your service will be disconnected on [Date, e.g., 14 days from now or end of current billing cycle]. Is that acceptable?

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: Yes, that date works. What about my equipment? Do I need to send back the modem and router, and how does the final bill work?

messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: Yes, you will need to return your equipment. I'll send you an email with detailed instructions and a pre-paid shipping label. You can drop it off at any UPS store. As for your final bill, it will be prorated up to your disconnect date. Any equipment not returned within 21 days will incur a charge. You'll receive your final statement within a week or two after your service ends.

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: Okay, got it. Thank you, Sarah.

messages.speaker: Agent
messages.text: You're welcome. Your cancellation request has been processed, and you'll receive a confirmation email shortly with all the details and your equipment return information. Thank you for being an AT&T customer, and please let us know if you decide to come back in the future. Have a good day.

messages.speaker: Customer
messages.text: No, that's everything. Thanks again. You too.
